title: Improved Baselines with Representation Autoencoders
abstract: Representation Autoencoders (RAE) replace traditional VAE with pretrained vision encoders. This paper studies design choices that simplify and improve RAE: aggregating the last k encoder layers rather than only the final layer, combining RAE with REPA because they have complementary mechanisms, and reformulating REPA as x-prediction in RAE latent space to provide internal guidance without a separate weaker model. RAEv2 achieves more than 10x faster convergence over the original RAE, reaching guided gFID 1.06 in 80 epochs on ImageNet-256 and EP_FID@2 of 35 epochs versus 177 for the original RAE. The paper also motivates training efficiency as a practical evaluation metric and validates the recipe on text-to-image generation and navigation world models.
publication_status: arXiv preprint; no formal conference or journal reference found as of 2026-06-08
